programme.title: Nadiya's Time to Eat
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her favourite recipes for when she wants to whip up something sweet and delicious, but deceptively simple. Colourful milkshake funnel cakes have all the fun of fresh doughnuts with none of the fuss. A giant time-saving cookie is so easy it's fried in the pan, and a glistening nutty baklava looks so impressive but is completely fool-proof. On the road, Nadiya joins the team Tate and Lyle on the River Thames, to find out how they produce one of her favourite fast-tracks to flavour - golden syrup(n)

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: St Helens v Warrington Wolves (Kick-off 3.00pm). Mark Chapman presents live coverage of the showpiece fixture, which takes place at Wembley Stadium, as Saints take on the Wire for the famous trophy. St Helens will be confident their first Challenge Cup final appearance since their 2008 victory will be a successful one given their domination of Super League this season. Hooker James Roby is the only survivor from that Saints success and he was in vintage form during their 26-2 semi-final win over Halifax. But Warrington are unlikely to go down without a fight, and have been St Helens' closest rivals through much of the domestic league campaign. However, since their Challenge Cup victory in 2012 they have lost their last six consecutive finals in league and cup. Can the likes of key man Blake Austin see them end that losing run at Wembley this weekend?(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: The team examines an 18th-century portrait attributed to pioneering female artist Maria Cosway, but which Philip Mould believes may actually be an undiscovered work by the great Regency artist Sir Thomas Lawrence. He noticed it during a visit to the owners' home and now the time has come to put his hunch to the test. What's more, the difference in value is enormous - as a work by Cosway, it is worth about £8,000. If the presenter is right, it could be more in the region of half a million(n)

programme.title: The Animal's Guide to Britain
programme.desc: Chris Packham examines Britain from an animal's point of view as he tries to understand the needs, problems and histories of native species. He begins with freshwater creatures, visiting the Highlands, where he witnesses a fishing display by ospreys and learns how to tempt them to nest further south. He also investigates what white-faced darter dragonflies, black water voles and brown trout can reveal about the changing nature of waterways(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Six more robots compete in the second heat, with last year's champion Carbide returning to face brand new challengers Eruption, Aftershock, Crackers N Smash, Gabriel 2 and Big Nipper. Dara Ó Briain and Angela Scanlon present the show, with commentary from Jonathan Pearce(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Raymond's bull Major returns to work after being injured earlier in the year, but getting him to the field of cows involves a soggy detour. Raymond also travels to Switzerland with fiancee Jen to help their friends gather their valais blacknose sheep from the mountaintops. Kirsty and Aimee go sheep shopping to double their flock, while bad news forces buffalo farmer Stevie to make a shocking decision(n)

programme.title: Top of the Shop with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Makers of cooking sauces compete against each other for a place in the final, as they test out their products on locals in Malhamdale, in the heart of the Yorkshire Dales. Joe, a teacher from Runcorn, spices things up with his home made hot sauce, while Spanish-born Beatriz makes her 'paella in a bottle'. Alice sells a salted caramel sauce made in her kitchen in Northern Ireland, and Kiran from Solihull offers up her healthy take on curry sauce(n)

programme.title: Secret Britain
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Adam Henson explore the secrets of the Scottish Highlands. Adam climbs the north face of Ben Nevis, a spectacular rock ascent on which few tourists venture, and lives out his boyhood dream on a Wild West-style cattle ranch. Ellie probes a monster mystery in Britain's deepest water Loch Morar, visits an ancient shrine to healing on the Black Isle, and teams up with Adam to go hunting for Bonnie Prince Charlie's cave hideaway, before they take a magical ride on the real Hogwarts Express(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin follow the progress of auction properties in Folkestone in Kent, Hanley, Stoke-on-Trent and Seascale in Cumbria(n)

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti work at the Royal Mansour in Marrakesh, Morocco, which was built by royal decree to showcase the region to world leaders, as well as billionaires and celebrity guests. Monica works with executive housekeeper Laurence, who shows her each of the 350 pairs of curtains must be caressed by hand to create perfect pleats, while Giles dons the uniform of the hotel butlers as he is introduced him to the hotel's underground tunnels, which ensure absolute discretion to the guests(n)

programme.title: Galapagos
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin and the team journey south to the oldest islands in the Galapagos to see the impact humans have had on this pristine wilderness. Moving on to the island of Isabela, Liz descends into a vertical lava cave, then makes her final land-based stop on Santa Cruz, where she meets a man on a mission to protect the giant tortoise population. Finally, she dives into the deep blue waters to witness the birth of a new island(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland. With analysis by Martin Keown and Karen Carney(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: On one of the hottest days of the year, the Dover RNLI crew race to a stricken Channel swimmer enduring difficulties within eyesight of the French coast. In Mudeford, there are growing fears for a dinghy sailor four miles out in rough seas and refusing to accept help. And the Tenby team are called to a runaway yacht which is on the verge of running aground(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Road to Mexico
programme.desc: The chef's latest culinary travels take him through Mexico City, and Puebla, where he samples Mexico's most iconic dish, the meat stew mole. Later, in the deserts of Oaxaca, he discovers how one of the country's most famous drinks, mezcal is made(n)

programme.title: Proms Encore
programme.desc: Katie Derham is joined by tenor Allan Clayton, soprano Nadine Benjamin and beatboxer Jason Singh as the programme focuses on 'the voice'. Tenor Jeffrey Lloyd-Roberts investigates different singing styles and Tom Service takes maestro Semyon Bychkov on a musical car journey in an Austin Maestro. There's also a genre-mixing finale as Jason samples Nadine's voice for a bandstand performance(n)

programme.title: International Rugby Union
programme.desc: England v Ireland. John Inverdale presents action from the World Cup warm-up fixture, which took place at Twickenham, as Ireland looked to avenge the 32-20 defeat they suffered against England on the opening weekend of the 2019 Six Nations in Dublin. With analysis from Jeremy Guscott(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Captain Mainwaring and his platoon are challenged to a cricket match by Warden Hodges, who has signed up a professional for his side. Featuring a guest appearance by former England and Yorkshire fast bowler Fred Trueman. Vintage comedy first shown in 1970, starring Arthur Lowe and Bill Pertwee(n)

programme.title: Viceroy's House

programme.title: Hunky Dory

Undeterred by general disinterest and sweltering heat, she sets out to inspire her class to stage a rock musical based on Shakespeare's The Tempest. Comedy drama, with Minnie Driver and Aneurin Barnard(n)

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter T, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With the bank holiday weekend looming, Monty Don has plenty of inspiration for gardeners from planting for late summer colour to tidying up herbs and pruning soft fruit. Nick Bailey concludes his journey into the clematis world by looking at the late summer-flowering varieties and the programme travels to Devon to meet a retired biology teacher with a passion for houseleeks(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher visits the Lake District to take a look at some of the jobs working animals do. He joins the Cockermouth Mountain Rescue team to see search-and-rescue dogs in action and meets 15-week-old Jura, a border collie pup learning the ropes from the older dogs. He then meets George Newton and his two Dales ponies, Charlie and George. They work the forests for timber as they're able to get to places that normal machinery can't. But Sean is surprised to find that the biggest job is being done by the smallest creatures - beetles(n)

programme.title: The Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Kirsty Wilson shows how to create an evergreen hanging basket, George Anderson takes a look at the bedding flower display and Carole Baxter starts sowing over the winter vegetables(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Nigel Slater: Eating Together
programme.desc: Nigel visits three home cooks to learn about well-loved soup recipes that have come from around the world. Miles tells him how his mother brought her recipe for spicy fish tea with her when she moved to Wiltshire from Jamaica in the 1960s, while Sumiko gives him a lesson in the art of Japanese miso soup. Finally, Nigel meets 87-year-old Regina at her home in Leeds to find out how she makes a classic Jewish chicken soup, joining her family for a Friday night Shabbat dinner(n)

programme.title: Top of the Shop with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Four more producers with fledgling businesses test out their products on the locals in Malhamdale, in the heart of the Yorkshire Dales. This time it's the turn of meat producers fighting it out for a place in the final. They include oil-rig worker Mark, who prepares and cures bacon from home in Aberdeenshire(n)

programme.title: International Rugby Union
programme.desc: England v Ireland. With analysis from Jeremy Guscott(n)

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: The Meeting de Paris. Action from the 12th meeting of the season, held at Stade Sebastien Charlety, where sprint star Noah Lyles was among the elite athletes expected to be on show(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents live coverage from Alexandra Stadium in Birmingham, where athletes compete for national honours and places in the squad for this year's World Championships in Doha. Dina Asher-Smith is expected to be a medal hope in Qatar in both the 100m and 200m, and competes in the latter today, while another global contender in Laura Muir is set to run in the 1500m(n)

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti travel more than 120 miles north of the Arctic Circle into Swedish Lapland to work in Icehotel in the village of Jukkasjarvi. Using two-ton blocks of ice hacked from the nearby River Torne, Giles works with a team of international artists to create individually crafted rooms and is taught how to make drinking glasses from ice, while Monica learns how the head chef uses natural ingredients found in the nearby forest; and meets Manne, an 82-year-old man who supplies reindeer meat to the hotel(n)

programme.title: The Ganges with Sue Perkins
programme.desc: The comedian and presenter embarks on an epic journey along the sacred river, from its source in the Himalayas to the Bay of Bengal. She begins by trekking to the source of the Ganges River in the highest mountain range on Earth, meeting hermits and wandering holy people that call this sacred landscape home. Moving on, she visits Rishikesh - a magnet for spiritual-seekers ever since the Beatles stayed there almost 50 years ago - meets a holy man-turned-business guru and attends a nightly ceremony to praise the river(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a mattress factory in Leeds, where he discovers how steel is stretched and coiled into springs and the advantages of natural fibres like hemp and wool. Cherry Healey talks to a sleep scientist about the benefits to reaction time from taking an afternoon nap, and Ruth Goodman tries out a Middle Ages mattress made out of a straw-stuffed sack(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman and Tej Lalvani assess the profit-making potential of an eco-friendly entrepreneur's alternative to straws, a range of skincare products, a device designed to make bikes more visible, and a brand of food with a personal story behind it. Evan Davis presents(n)

programme.title: A Black and White Killing: The Case That Shook America
programme.desc: Part one of two. Mobeen Azhar investigates the murder of Larnell Bruce by a member of a white supremacist gang in Portland, and assesses the degree to which race played a role in the choice of victim. The investigation delves into the history of the seemingly liberal city, and uncovers a dark underbelly of racial tensions in the state of Oregon. The programme also examines the events of the trial, which were marked by clashes between anti-fascist groups and white supremacists on the streets. Featuring interviews with members of the militant groups and expert witnesses called on to testify(n)

programme.title: Peter Taylor: My Journey Through the Troubles
programme.desc: Journalist Peter Taylor presents a documentary marking the 50th anniversary of the deployment of British troops in August 1969. Peter reflects on his experiences of covering the Northern Ireland conflict for almost a half century. Peter has written a number of books on political violence, exploring the Troubles from British, Loyalist and Republican points of view. This film is a highly personal account of the key events of the Troubles and the enduring legacies of the conflict, drawing on Peter's experiences of reporting from the region(n)

programme.title: Pioneer
programme.desc: An American oil company plans to build an underwater pipeline off the coast of Norway, and hires a deep-sea diver to work on the project. However, when the operation is struck by a tragic accident, he becomes suspicious of his employers' true objectives. Thriller, starring Aksel Hennie, Wes Bentley and Stephen Lang. In Norwegian and English(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actress Naomie Harris's dramatic discoveries in this edition of the show in which celebrities research their family trees inspires her to anger, sadness and tears of joy. On a journey from Trinidad to Grenada to Jamaica, she learns of her great-great-great-great-grandfather's involvement in the exploitation of so-called liberated Africans shortly after the abolition of slavery, is delighted to discover the identity of her African five-times-great grandmother, and uncovers a family story of tragic poverty in Jamaica's capital of Kingston(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Essie has a shocking encounter with a distressed stranger who claims to know Raf, and plays on her good nature - leading her to make a pact that could change her life for ever. Xav notices Ric making more and more mistakes, and decides to confront him about it. Kian tries to boost Nicky's confidence by giving her a tricky patient to diagnose(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: A Matter of Life and Debt
programme.desc: In Bridge of Allan, Karen and her 75-year-old father go into business together combining the ancient craft of glassblowing and Scotland's famous tipple, creating an unique range of whisky gifts. With the aid of a £25,000 loan the business is continuing to keep a dying art alive. Plus, a transgender entrepreneur takes control of her future after struggling with employment(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle observe events as rare Amur tiger Yana goes into labour, meaning the Park's first cubs for nearly two decades are imminent. Kate also catches up on life in the gorilla house with the focus on silverback Kesho, while Jean Johansson discovers why coconuts are just the job for the dental health of Cape Porcupines(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a croissant factory in France that produces 336,000 pastries a day, and discovers how an 83-year-old strain of yeast is used to create the perfect flavour. Cherry Healey talks to a professor who specialises in exploring human senses about the perfect way to eat a croissant and visits a farm that produces concentrated butter that is 99.8 percent fat. Plus, Ruth Goodman is in Paris investigating the croissant's surprising Austrian origins and the role of bread in the French revolution(n)

programme.title: BBC News at 9
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Equestrian
programme.desc: Action from Rotterdam, where British Olympic gold medallists Charlotte Dujardin, Scott Brash and Ben Maher were among those expected to be competing(n)

programme.title: The Muppets
programme.desc: Three fans of the famous puppet variety show discover a powerful businessman is going to knock down the Muppet theatre and drill for oil on the site. They track down frontman Kermit the frog and persuade him to reunite his co-stars, so they can put on one last performance to save the venue. Comedy, starring Jason Segel, Amy Adams and Chris Cooper, with songs by Bret McKenzie (Flight of the Conchords)(n)

credits.actor: Jason Segel, Amy Adams, Chris Cooper, songs by Bret McKenzie (Flight of the Conchords)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: Michael Buerk helps search for heirs to Alexander Gibb's substantial estate, while a surprising family secret is discovered while delving into the case of Robert Heavens(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers continue their culinary history of British food by exploring breakfast dishes, revealing how the morning meal keeps evolving(n)

programme.title: World's Sneakiest Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham features a host of animals that use deceptive tactics to find food. Some trick their prey, like the manipulative mantis which looks like a flower and killer whales which set traps using sound. Others cheat each other - sea otters demand ransoms after kidnapping each other's pups - and some, like African bird the drongo and monkeys, fool other species. In parks, meanwhile, grey squirrels are at war in stealing raids and it seems these animal liars, cheats and hustlers really will stop at nothing to get a meal(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Charlie Ross head out from Sevenoaks to an auction at Folkestone, learning the story of the Kent cobnut harvest along the way(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the first leg of a journey from the coalfields of South Wales to the southernmost tip of Cornwall, travelling from Whitland, Carmarthenshire, to Swansea(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Science and technology experts Spencer Kelly, Dr Helen Lawal, Dallas Campbell, Ajay Tegala and Rory Cellan-Jones take on the general-knowledge champions, hoping to win a cash prize for their chosen charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four more treasured family heirlooms, and the memories they hold, back to life, tackling a radio that survived Dunkirk, a battered leather gaming case, a grandfather clock with a poignant history, and a pair of Viking statues(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Summer Feasts
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain prepares an array of mouth-watering dishes that she will serve up to her loved ones at her own summer party feast, including a barbecued butterflied leg of lamb with sticky rhubarb glaze. She makes side dishes of kiwi and feta salad and edamame wild rice, as well as olive and rosemary bread shaped like a crown. For dessert, she goes all out with a magical princess torte cake and an ingenious raspberry ripple eclair, and creates a thirst-quenching drink in the form of an elderflower cooler(n)

programme.title: A Black and White Killing: The Case That Shook America
programme.desc: Part two of two. Mobeen Azhar seeks to understand the complications surrounding the lives and behaviour of both perpetrator Russell Courtier and victim Larnell Bruce, looking more closely into the nature and extent of European Kindred's influence on the wider community. He investigates the brainwashing effect that membership of Oregon's racist prison gangs can have on young men, before hearing the jury deliver its verdict on whether Courtier is a racist murderer(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: The second of two editions celebrating LGBT+ stand-up comedy at the BBC, showcasing the funniest moments from stars who have appeared on Live at the Apollo. This edition features sets from Tom Allen, Stephen K Amos, Jen Brister, Al Porter, Julian Clary, Suzi Ruffell and Gina Yashere(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Animated duo Beavis and Butt-Head reflect on a year in which conspiracy theories appeared left, right and centre as The X Files hit TV screens, and Mr Blobby began his illustrious career on Noel's House Party. Boy band rivalry appeared as East 17 embarked on their ill-fated mission to give Take That a run for their money and John Wayne Bobbitt came to know the true meaning of loss(n)

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Steve Wright narrates a selection of appearances and promo videos taken from long-running music show Top of the Pops during the 1990s, including performances by Spice Girls, Michael Jackson, Pulp, the Prodigy, Madonna, Blur and Take That. First broadcast on New Year's Eve 1999(n)

programme.title: Lost Land of the Volcano
programme.desc: With the team's explorations of New Guinea drawing to a close, George McGavin visits an erupting volcano, where he encounters a rare bird that depends on the hot ash to survive. Back at the original site, Steve Backshall and Gordon Buchanan head deep into the crater and discover two new mammals - a giant rat and a tree-climbing marsupial(n)

programme.title: The Private Life of Cows
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty reveals the hidden lives of farmyard animals, beginning with an investigation into the nature of cows. Visiting Devon and Northumberland, he discovers how the hierarchy of a herd is established, tests their intelligence by trying to teach three animals how to demand food using a bell, and discovers what could turn a placid creature into a potential killer(n)

programme.title: Below the Surface
programme.desc: SP and Simon finally have a lead on the whereabouts of June's phone, while Philip is told that the hostages have been booby-trapped - with Yusuf threatening to detonate. In Danish(n)

programme.title: Below the Surface
programme.desc: As the ferry docks in Provestenen, Beate greets Philip - who sees June chasing Rami in the distance. Grabbing a gun he follows them into a dockside warehouse. In Danish(n)

programme.title: The Vietnam War
programme.sub-title: This Is What We Do (July 1967 - December 1967)
programme.desc: Hanoi lays plans for a massive surprise offensive, while the Johnson administration attempts to reassure the American public that victory is in sight(n)

programme.title: The Vietnam War
programme.desc: On the eve of the Tet holiday, North Vietnamese and Viet Cong forces launch surprise attacks throughout the south, while Lyndon Johnson decides not to stand for re-election(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Simon Bates and Gary Davies introduce the edition first aired on May 19, featuring Prefab Sprout, Kylie Minogue, Derek B, Aztec Camera, Heart, Ofra Haza, Hothouse Flowers, Debbie Gibson, Belinda Carlisle, Billy Bragg with Cara Tivey, Wet Wet Wet and Liverpool FC(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The gripping drama is halfway through and the twists and turns come thick and fast as Tommy shares some important family news with his brothers, and outlines his plans for the most audacious criminal act the gang has ever undertaken. He also finds himself playing a dangerous game with Tatiana as they attempt to acquire information from each another. Meanwhile, a guilty Polly makes an ill-advised confession - with terrible consequences for her nephew. Cillian Murphy stars, with Helen McCrory, Paddy Considine and Gaite Jansen(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The Russians test the Shelbys and their gang, and Tommy begins to realise he may have underestimated his new business partners, and has now been seriously outmanoeuvred. As aristocrats exiled by the Soviet Revolution, the Petrovnas have absolutely nothing to lose, and beneath their civilised veneer lies a fierce determination to regain what they have lost. However, the Birmingham mobster still has an ace up his sleeve, in the form of an old enemy now working as an ally. The question is, can he control him? Tom Hardy returns as menacing mobster Alfie Solomons, with Cillian Murphy, Helen McCrory, Annabelle Wallis and Paul Anderson(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The third run of Steven Knight's period drama reaches its final episode, and yet again Cillian Murphy as Thomas Shelby finds himself in a seemingly impossible situation. Has his criminal life finally caught up with the Birmingham-based mobster? Tommy prepares to commit the most audacious crime of his career, only to receive an unexpected blow that puts him under unbearable pressure. In a nerve-shredding race against time, Tommy is forced to confront hard truths about himself and his family that could change the Peaky Blinders for ever. As he faces his worst fears he needs help more than ever - but is increasingly uncertain about who he can trust(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.desc: Sheku Kanneh-Mason performs Elgar's Cello Concerto at the Royal Albert Hall, with the City of Birmingham Symphony Orchestra and their Music Director Mirga Gražinyte-Tyla. Introduced by Tom Service and Sheku's sister, pianist Isata Kanneh-Mason(n)

programme.title: Peter Rice: An Engineer Imagines
programme.desc: A tribute to the life and ideas of Peter Rice, one of the most distinguished structural engineers of the late 20th century. The film traces his career from his native Ireland through London, Sydney and Paris, revealing how he pushed the boundaries of art and science, until to his untimely death in 1992(n)

programme.title: Foo Fighters at Reading 2019
programme.desc: Huw Stephens introduces highlights from the headline set, as the Foo Fighters run through dozens of classic hits from their back catalogue(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James concludes his examination of the social significance of cars by exploring how aspiration and new wealth were behind the development of some of the greatest models ever made. He tries to make sense of the baffling world of company car hierarchy by holding a travelling salesman race-off and indulges his 1980s urges with the twin delights of a Lamborghini and a Porsche. He also unveils his choice of the ultimate people's car - a vehicle that can lay claim to being the greatest in history(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy receives a mysterious letter on Christmas Eve 1925 and realises that the Shelbys are in danger of annihilation, despite his new-found and unprecedented legitimacy. As the enemy closes in, Tommy flees his country house and returns to the only safe place he knows - Small Heath, Birmingham, where the family must find a way to put differences aside, take up arms and fight for survival(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: As the Shelbys come to terms with the shocking events of Christmas Day, Tommy tries to unite his family and makes a decision he may come to regret, enlisting the help of tough Romany Gypsy Aberama Gold, who wants something unusual in return. Jessie Eden confronts Tommy about the workers' pay, warning him that revolution is in the air. As the situation plays out, Tommy's factory manager tells him he has one more meeting, with a mysterious businessman from Paris, but what transpires is no ordinary meeting, with no ordinary businessman(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The Italians launch another attack on the Peaky Blinders and Tommy realises that the Shelbys need to evolve if they are to survive, but some of the family are reluctant to part with tradition. As the strike takes hold at the Lanchester factory, Tommy pays a personal visit to Jessie Eden, but is outmanoeuvred when she reveals something she knows about his past, while Changretta plans further ways to continue the vendetta in the most devastating way possible. Elsewhere, Luca makes direct contact with someone at the heart of the Peaky Blinders organisation(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Building Cars: Secrets of the Assembly Line
programme.desc: IPart one of two. James May, Kate Humble and Ant Anstead broadcast from inside Mini Plant Oxford, one of Britain's biggest and busiest car factories, where a new car drives off the production line every 68 seconds. James looks at the use of robotic technology and investigates the claim that British manufacturing is dead, while Ant explores the history and heritage of one of the most iconic cars ever built, and Kate reports on the use of electric cars in Norway(n)

programme.title: Victoria & Albert: The Royal Wedding
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley oversees a restaging of the wedding of Queen Victoria and Prince Albert. Aided by food historian Annie Gray, clothing expert Harriet Waterhouse and military historian Jasdeep Singh, she recreates the most important elements of the ceremony and the celebrations, scouring history books, archives, newspapers and Victoria's diaries for the exact details of the couple's big day in 1840. Lucy also recounts the story of Victoria and Albert's courtship and engagement, exploring the hidden iconography and symbolism of this hugely significant wedding and revealing new insights into the pair's relationship, both public and private(n)

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: The focus turns to apes and monkeys, revealing their similarities to humans. Large armies of hamadryas baboons battle on the plains of Ethiopia to steal females and settle old scores, while Japanese macaques beat the cold by lounging in thermal springs. An orang-utan baby fails in its struggle to make an umbrella out of leaves, and capuchins try to smash nuts with a large rock. Narrated by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The Shelbys are lured by the Italians into a cat-and-mouse chase on the street of Birmingham, where it becomes clear that Tommy has met his match. Tommy tries to console himself with a visit from an old flame, but it soon becomes clear that he cannot always get what he wants. As his factory lies idle, Tommy confronts the possibility that the Communists might win and he'll be deemed a traitor to his class. Meanwhile, Changretta prepares to spring another trap. Period gangster drama, starring Cillian Murphy(n)

programme.title: The Worst Witch
programme.desc: Mildred's having fun jumping high into the air with Enid - using pirated magic to make the grass bouncy. Maud's getting fed up with Enid constantly distracting Mildred - Maud knows Mildred needs to knuckle down if she's going to stand a chance at the academy and can't bear the thought of losing her. Mildred receives a singing teddy from home and it makes her feel homesick. When Mildred looks to Maud for help in Miss Gullet's transformations class, Maud doesn't bail her out and Mildred turns Ethel into a pig instead of a mouse into frog. She doesn't even manage to turn Ethel back successfully - she still has a pig's snout! Mildred receives a harsh warning from Miss Hardbroom who threatens expulsion if she can't perform the spell correctly. Mildred feels fed up with all the rules of school and always being the worst witch. It's the final straw, she wants to go home. programme.title: The Worst Witch
programme.desc: Everyone's surprised to hear the Great Wizard is making an unexpected visit to Cackle's Academy. He's had a tip off that the school is going rapidly downhill and it looks like non-magical Mildred is at the heart of the problems. Miss Cackle (really Agatha) refuses to expel Mildred and waxes lyrical about her. The Great Wizard insists that Mildred should perform a magical display to prove herself. Creative Mildred conducts a magical potions choir, but it ends in disaster leaving Miss Cackle on thin ice. To everyone's surprise, Miss Cackle (Agatha) makes Mildred lead a broomstick display. Mildred works out that Agatha is impersonating Miss Cackle but has to go ahead with the display - not realising Ethel had put a spell on the broomstick. Mildred begs Maud to find the real Miss Cackle, so while Mildred and Enid are in peril in the air, Maud battles her way up to Miss Cackle's room to find her under an entrancement spell. Just as Mildred and Enid start to plummet, the real Miss Cackle sav(n)

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: St Helens v Warrington Wolves (Kick-off 3.00pm). Mark Chapman presents live coverage of the showpiece fixture, which takes place at Wembley Stadium, as Saints take on the Wire for the famous trophy. St Helens will be confident their first Challenge Cup final appearance since their 2008 victory will be a successful one given their domination of Super League this season. Hooker James Roby is the only survivor from that Saints success and he was in vintage form during their 26-2 semi-final win over Halifax. But Warrington are unlikely to go down without a fight, and have been St Helens' closest rivals through much of the domestic league campaign. However, since their Challenge Cup victory in 2012 they have lost their last six consecutive finals in league and cup. Can the likes of key man Blake Austin see them end that losing run at Wembley this weekend?(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Arsenal at Anfield and Manchester United v Crystal Palace at Old Trafford. Jurgen Klopp's side had an excellent recent record against the Gunners, having gone undefeated against them since April 2015. Last season's corresponding fixture saw the Reds run out 5-1 winners, with a Roberto Firmino hat-trick doing most of the damage. Plus, action from Brighton & Hove Albion v Southampton at the Amex Stadium, Norwich City v Chelsea at Carrow Road, Sheffield United v Leicester City at Bramall Lane, Watford v West Ham United at Vicarage Road and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Burnley at Molineux. She begins in North Wales, trying to climb the tallest man-made structure in the country to fix some loose cables on the Arfon Transmission Tower. Next, Kate learns how to overcome natural obstacles with the novel activity of packrafting - using a lightweight inflatable canoe. programme.desc: Bill Paterson narrates highlights of the event held on the esplanade of Edinburgh Castle, while Aasmah Mir goes behind-the-scenes to meet some of the performers and get a flavour of how this enormous event comes together. programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Whitney finds Callum's letters from Chris and questions him, but he cannot bring himself to tell her the truth. programme.title: Poldark
programme.desc: Several months have passed and Ross has got in deeper with the French invasion force as they prepare to welcome their leader General Toussaint. Meanwhile, Morwenna and Drake are ready to welcome their baby, while George stumbles on the means to undo Ross once and for all with the help of Merceron and Hanson. Ross and his friends must launch their most daring plan yet to try to save themselves and the country from enemies both at home and abroad. Last-ever episode of the drama, starring Aidan Turner(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Time to Eat
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her favourite recipes for when she wants to whip up something sweet and delicious, but deceptively simple. Colourful milkshake funnel cakes have all the fun of fresh doughnuts with none of the fuss. A giant time-saving cookie is so easy it's fried in the pan, and a glistening nutty baklava looks so impressive but is completely fool-proof. On the road, Nadiya joins the team Tate and Lyle on the River Thames, to find out how they produce one of her favourite fast-tracks to flavour - golden syrup(n)

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: St Helens v Warrington Wolves (Kick-off 3.00pm). Mark Chapman presents live coverage of the showpiece fixture, which takes place at Wembley Stadium, as Saints take on the Wire for the famous trophy. St Helens will be confident their first Challenge Cup final appearance since their 2008 victory will be a successful one given their domination of Super League this season. Hooker James Roby is the only survivor from that Saints success and he was in vintage form during their 26-2 semi-final win over Halifax. But Warrington are unlikely to go down without a fight, and have been St Helens' closest rivals through much of the domestic league campaign. However, since their Challenge Cup victory in 2012 they have lost their last six consecutive finals in league and cup. Can the likes of key man Blake Austin see them end that losing run at Wembley this weekend?(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Arsenal at Anfield and Manchester United v Crystal Palace at Old Trafford. Jurgen Klopp's side had an excellent recent record against the Gunners, having gone undefeated against them since April 2015. Last season's corresponding fixture saw the Reds run out 5-1 winners, with a Roberto Firmino hat-trick doing most of the damage. Plus, action from Brighton & Hove Albion v Southampton at the Amex Stadium, Norwich City v Chelsea at Carrow Road, Sheffield United v Leicester City at Bramall Lane, Watford v West Ham United at Vicarage Road and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Burnley at Molineux. With analysis by Ian Wright and Danny Murphy(n)

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: Seven unsigned MCs compete to become the next rap superstar, as they go head-to-head in a battle to sign for rap legends Krept and Konan's record label. Each week the artists are set two tests to find out who has the strongest song writing props and stage presence. In the first week, the contestants are challenged to rap over the beat that made Krept and Konan household names(n)

programme.desc: Claire McCollum explores the Yorkshire Dales on a motortrike and meets the Christian woman behind the tours, and also visits Wensleydale Creamery to learn about the cheese(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: Edith and Phil are determined to fulfill their dream of retiring to the sun together, but find themselves continually hampered by logistical difficulties. Edith is finding her irritating sister Joan very difficult to accommodate, and Roger, impatient to see Phil sell his house, decides to help the process along by checking his roof for dry rot. Wendy drops in on Edith and mentions that she is planning a children's book, and despite not yet knowing what the plotline will be, thinks it may involve a complicated family. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese and Sue Johnston(n)

credits.actor: Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese, Sue Johnston

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: Edith and Phil discover a bookshop hosting local author Wendy Stevens, conducting a signing of her inaugural book, The Lemming Family. However, Phil is later outraged to find out that the characters are thinly disguised portraits of himself and the family. Roger's hopes of becoming a Social Services-supported carer look like being revived, though not quite in the way he imagines when the infamous Mrs Pool makes a reappearance. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese and Sue Johnston(n)

credits.actor: Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese, Sue Johnston

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern travel to Leigh-on-Sea, Essex, to meet Antonietta Tulino and her three children, hoping to help the family change their spending habits. In an effort to confront their money-spending ways, Alex and Steph resort to shock tactics, and set up a pop-up-shop in the family home displaying all of their biggest spends, with shocking results. Can Alex and Steph help this family start saving, rather than spending?(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: The team examines an 18th-century portrait attributed to pioneering female artist Maria Cosway, but which Philip Mould believes may actually be an undiscovered work by the great Regency artist Sir Thomas Lawrence. He noticed it during a visit to the owners' home and now the time has come to put his hunch to the test. What's more, the difference in value is enormous - as a work by Cosway, it is worth about £8,000. If the presenter is right, it could be more in the region of half a million(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a 1980s edition of the celebrity quiz, featuring four teams of famous faces from the decade seeking unlikely answers. The contestants are athletes Fatima Whitbread and Robin Cousins, Doctor Who stars Colin Baker and Nicola Bryant, Blue Peter presenters Mark Curry and Peter Duncan, and singers Sonia and Jaki Graham(n)

St Helens will be confident their first Challenge Cup final appearance since their 2008 victory will be a successful one given their domination of Super League this season. Hooker James Roby is the only survivor from that Saints success and he was in vintage form during their 26-2 semi-final win over Halifax. But Warrington are unlikely to go down without a fight, and have been St Helens' closest rivals through much of the domestic league campaign. However, since their Challenge Cup victory in 2012 they have lost their last six consecutive finals in league and cup. Can the likes of key man Blake Austin see them end that losing run at Wembley this weekend?(n)

programme.desc: Edith and Phil are determined to fulfill their dream of retiring to the sun together, but find themselves continually hampered by logistical difficulties. programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: The team examines an 18th-century portrait attributed to pioneering female artist Maria Cosway, but which Philip Mould believes may actually be an undiscovered work by the great Regency artist Sir Thomas Lawrence. He noticed it during a visit to the owners' home and now the time has come to put his hunch to the test. What's more, the difference in value is enormous - as a work by Cosway, it is worth about £8,000. If the presenter is right, it could be more in the region of half a million(n)

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption argues that judges - especially those of the European Court of Human Rights - have usurped power by expanding the interpretation of human rights law. But by contrast, the European Convention on Human Rights has become a dynamic treaty, taking on new interpretations and powers(n)

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption assesses the US and UK's constitutional models. He describes Britain's unwritten constitution as a political institution. programme.title: Harry Hill's Alien Fun Capsule
programme.desc: Madcap panel show presented by the comedian, with Jordan Stephens, Janet Street-Porter, Alex Brooker and Una Stubbs nominally tasked with saving planet Earth from any potential alien invasion. They must find clips, people and anything that is fun or funny enough for Harry to place in the capsule which, in the event that the planet is invaded by aliens, can be presented to them to demonstrate earthlings are good fun and therefore worthy of saving(n)

programme.title: Harry Hill's Alien Fun Capsule
programme.desc: The big-collared comedian returns with another round of madcap fun and laughter, joined by Emmerdale actor Gaynor Faye, Countryfile presenter Anita Rani, Professor Robert Winston and comedy legends Cannon and Ball. Their job is supposedly to help Harry fill a capsule with jokes, games, songs and clips to prove just how funny the human race can be and prevent an alien invasion - but in actual fact it's just an excuse to let Harry bring his particular brand of comedy back to prime-time television(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill raids the archives to present the 100 funniest home videos. The countdown includes clips of a faulty safety rope, a boy who is afraid of falling from a fairground ride, and a cat who can lip sync to music(n)

programme.desc: Welsh comedian and presenter Griff Rhys Jones presents a compilation of never-seen-before out-takes from the world of film and TV. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

After rejecting the proposal, the sleuth wakes up the following morning to learn the man has been stabbed to death while the train was held up by heavy snow in Serbia. Racing against time to find the killer among the passengers, Poirot must look to the past to solve the crime, and is faced with a decision that will haunt him for ever. programme.title: Cilla
programme.desc: It looks like Cilla has put that early botched audition and those disappointing record sales behind her as, following the success of Anyone Who Had a Heart, her new single You're My World becomes her second number one. However, while she may have conquered the UK charts, can she follow in the footsteps of her old mates the Beatles and crack America? The signs aren't looking good, and just to add to the pressure, her relationship with Bobby is becoming increasingly tumultuous. But as her manager Brian Epstein's private life starts to spiral out of control, it seems there could be an even bigger threat to Cilla's new-found success. programme.desc: Robert has to make what could be a far-reaching decision about the recent bombshell. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

After rejecting the proposal, the sleuth wakes up the following morning to learn the man has been stabbed to death while the train was held up by heavy snow in Serbia. Racing against time to find the killer among the passengers, Poirot must look to the past to solve the crime, and is faced with a decision that will haunt him for ever. However, while she may have conquered the UK charts, can she follow in the footsteps of her old mates the Beatles and crack America? The signs aren't looking good, and just to add to the pressure, her relationship with Bobby is becoming increasingly tumultuous. But as her manager Brian Epstein's private life starts to spiral out of control, it seems there could be an even bigger threat to Cilla's new-found success. programme.desc: Robert has to make what could be a far-reaching decision about the recent bombshell. Spy adventure, with Pierce Brosnan taking on his first assignment as 007. Sean Bean, Izabella Scorupco and Famke Janssen also star. programme.desc: A chance to see day two of the inaugural staging of the event from Claudelands Arena in Hamilton, featuring the quarter-finals, semi-finals and final, all played over the best of 15 legs. programme.title: Prince Albert: A Victorian Hero Revealed
programme.desc: Professor Saul David examines Prince Albert's significant influence on British culture, governmental policy and even international relations in his short life. The queen's consort faced an uphill struggle to be taken seriously by a nation and parliament that viewed him with hostility, but by the time of his death at the age of 42, he was widely regarded as a forward-thinking reformer. The programme draws on the private papers in the Royal Archives to gain a full picture of his legacy(n)

programme.title: Prince Philip: The Plot to Make a King
programme.desc: Secret History documentary telling the inside story of the dynastic tensions that were unleashed when Princess Elizabeth, the future queen, fell in love with Prince Philip. It was felt that Philip was rough and ill-mannered - and with the Second World War still fresh in most people's minds, the royal and political elite disliked his German roots. But following the death of King George VI in 1952, the establishment feared a dynastic coup, with the Queen torn between the two sides. The documentary examines the complexities and frustrations of the royal marriage, as the monarch struggled to reconcile the love of her husband with the suspicions of her family and her government(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Lost Family

programme.title: The Secret World of Your Rubbish
programme.desc: Documentary following people around the UK working in waste disposal. The first episode features the owners of a landfill site in Wiltshire, who employ salvagers to find anything that can be reused, London refuse collectors, who reveal the difficulty of dealing with contaminated recycling, and the supervisor at a skip hire company, who has worked for the business since he was 11(n)

programme.title: The Secret World of Your Rubbish
programme.desc: Workers at Crapper and Sons salvage what they can from the hundreds of tons of plastic that is brought in every month while 10,000 tons of compost is sold to local farmers every year. S Norton is a multi-million pound complex of hydraulic and electrical systems, where hundreds of people shred 1.5 million tons of steel per year, which is then shipped off to Turkey for use in steelworks around the world. Today(n)

programme.title: The Secret World of Your Rubbish
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on the work of Refood, which diverts rotting food from landfill and plugs it into the National Grid. If all the food wasted by UK homes was turned into energy, it could power more than 1.2 million homes. There is also a look at Sue and John Wellfare, who have made it their mission to recycle the unrecyclable. The pair sort items by hand, from biscuit wrappers and baby-food sachets to crisp packets, and have set up drop-off points for what would otherwise end up on the ground(n)

programme.title: A Bridge Too Far

Former staff reveal how Aldi has overcome shoppers' suspicions using a host of clever techniques to cut costs, from multiple barcodes and less time spent at the tills, to their own-brand products - which are intended to look and taste like their big-name counterparts, but cost a fraction of the price(n)

programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty investigates supermarkets' stringent criteria for the perfect vegetable, and the incredible lengths to which farmers go to grow produce that looks more attractive. In America, Kate Quiton examines why wine is getting stronger and learns about a machine that has been removing excess alcohol from some Californian wines since the 1990s. programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts the topical comedy quiz, as guests Ed Byrne and Jack Whitehall join regular panellists Hugh Dennis and Andy Parsons to offer opinions on the week's world events in an edition from 2010(n)

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian tries out being a teacher at Monnow Primary School in Bettws, Gwent. However, 'Mr Gilbert' soon feels the pressure as he tries to impress his colleagues and the children in his class(n)

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian undertakes a work placement at the Welsh Mountain Zoo near Colwyn Bay, Conwy, where his tasks include looking after tigers, sea lions and monkeys. However, Rhod struggles to remember the exotic creatures' names, let alone how to take care of them(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Stand-up and actress Jo Brand takes over the reins for extended edition of the satirical quiz, with broadcaster and journalist Grace Dent and comedian Kiri Pritchard-McLean joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Ross Noble: Off Road
programme.desc: It's the last three days of the Scottish Six Days Trial, one of the world's toughest motorbike challenges(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The conclusion of the two-part special in which Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May take three used, V8-engined sports cars on a gruelling road trip covering 1,600 miles through Patagonia to the world's southernmost city. Having encountered stunning landscapes, mechanical mishaps and dreadful roads on their journey through Argentina, the trio's adventure is brought to a dramatic climax by a terrifying turn of events(n)

programme.title: Red Bull Soapbox Race
programme.desc: Highlights of the offbeat event, where teams create vehicles without engines and race downhill(n)

programme.title: Red Bull Soapbox Race
programme.sub-title: Best of Red Bull Soapbox Race
programme.desc: A compilation of the best karts, crashes and costumes from the most recent Red Bull Soapbox action from around the world(n)

programme.title: Lazy Boy Garage
programme.desc: Three best friends engaged in the world of international car ‘flipping' - where cars are quickly resold for profit in Lincolnshire. The opening episode focuses on Range Rovers(n)

programme.title: Lazy Boy Garage
programme.desc: Jonny Smith finds a two-door Ford Escort estate in Austria, but Tim Glover's worst fears are confirmed when it arrives, leaving him with the toughest of welding tasks to salvage it(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are sent to Bolivia, where they are challenged to travel through the rainforest and journey to the coast of Chile, driving second-hand off-road vehicles bought locally for a maximum of £3,500 each. Along the way, they encounter drug lords and feel the effects of altitude sickness as they negotiate the region's roads, one of which is a notoriously dangerous 40-mile mountain pass dubbed El Camino de la Muerte - the Death Road(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The conclusion of the two-part special in which Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May take three used, V8-engined sports cars on a gruelling road trip covering 1,600 miles through Patagonia to the world's southernmost city. Having encountered stunning landscapes, mechanical mishaps and dreadful roads on their journey through Argentina, the trio's adventure is brought to a dramatic climax by a terrifying turn of events(n)

programme.title: Red Bull Soapbox Race
programme.desc: Coverage of the Brazil event, in which teams of amateur racers design and build their own gravity-powered soapbox vehicles and race them in an extreme downhill battle(n)

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: At the halfway stage, all contestants - except Phil Wang - are in with a chance of victory. Kerry Godliman and Jessica Knappett resort to soapy violence(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.sub-title: Have I Got a Bit More Old News for You
programme.desc: Room 101 host Frank Skinner presides over an extended edition of the satirical quiz from June 2018, with playwright Lucy Prebble and comedian Henning Wehn joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: An extended edition of the unconventional quiz, in which Jason Manford, Jimmy Carr and Victoria Coren Mitchell join regular panellist Alan Davies to answer host Sandi Toksvig's questions on the theme of 'Oddballs'. During the course of the show, the panellists encounter some vegetarian goatsuckers. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window, as the fiendishly difficult questions lead to points being awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Dave Gorman: Modern Life Is Goodish
programme.sub-title: Like Top Gun All Over Again
programme.desc: Dave Gorman returns for another series of witty stand-up and wry observations on DVDs, baking and Bucks Fizz(n)

programme.title: Jon Richardson: Ultimate Worrier
programme.desc: Jon and guests Sara Pascoe, Phil Wang and Catherine Bohart discuss, categorise and rank worries to do with Global Affairs. Does the world hate Britain? What are micronations?(n)

programme.title: Napoleon
programme.desc: Historian Andrew Roberts marks the 200th anniversary of the Battle of Waterloo with a three-part series telling the definitive story of Napoleon Bonaparte, the Corsican Army officer who would eventually become the First Consul and later still Emperor of France. Travelling throughout Europe, and making use of some of the ill-fated dictator's personal letters, Andrew highlights Napoleon's early military successes in Italy and Egypt, as well as his role in pacifying anti-revolutionary activists in Paris during the 1790s, and his role as a progressive moderniser in the early years of his dictatorship(n)

programme.title: After Hitler
programme.desc: Part one of a two-part documentary about the aftermath of the Second World War, as Europe hoped to attain a lasting peace and refugees waited to return to their homelands(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough presents an exploration of Earth's vast seas, which cover 70 per cent of the planet's surface. In the first episode, millions of squid appear off the coast of California, gannets, whale sharks and dolphins hunt migrating sardines in the Indian Ocean, and thousands of turtles make the annual pilgrimage to lay their eggs in Costa Rica(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Camera crews journey to the deepest parts of the world's oceans to explore the lives of their inhabitants, including fearsome predators with huge teeth, strange jellyfish and deep-sea sharks that lurk among the gigantic submerged mountains and volcanos. Presented by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Predators and prey in the open oceans come under the scrutiny of David Attenborough. Marlin track down a shoal of sardines as a sei whale scoops up the leftovers, dolphins herd mackerel and attract the attention of a sailfish with the resonance of their sonar, and giant sunfish queue up to allow other species, including seagulls, to feed on their parasites(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Marine life at the poles, where winter temperatures frequently fall to a numbing -40C. Even when the sun reappears in the spring, its weak rays take a long time to melt the ice. David Attenborough describes how wildlife including seals, whales, polar bears and penguins overcome the elements to survive through prolonged cold spells(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: An exploration of the world's most densely inhabited waters, where variations in temperature owing to the year-long cycle of the sun encourage a vast array of marine life, from seals and sharks in the winter, to lobster and salmon in the summer, dolphins in the autumn and the walking handfish in spring. Narrated by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Footage of animals that live on the coast, including sea lions working in packs to hunt tuna in the shallows and moray eels that venture out of the water to seek out food on dry rocks. Puffins must brave predators to bring a mouthful of food back to their young and king penguins go without food for a month while they moult. Plus, footage of the bizarre Leaping Blenny, a fish that hates the water(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough investigates the opportunities tides create for marine life, showing giant stingrays gliding on currents for a rest, South African sea snails surfing the waves in pursuit of a meal, crabs playing football on a beach, and bottlenose dolphins embarking on a dangerous game of catch. In the oceanic world each turn of the tide can mean life or death for its inhabitants(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Exploring the wealth of marine life that inhabits the ocean's edge, dealing with the dangers of an ever-shifting environment. David Attenborough observes nesting seabirds in east Russia, Patagonian killer whales, Newfoundland caplin fish, walruses, and Brazilian green turtles - which make their annual expedition to an island in the middle of the Atlantic to lay their eggs(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough presents an exploration of Earth's vast seas, which cover 70 per cent of the planet's surface. In the first episode, millions of squid appear off the coast of California, gannets, whale sharks and dolphins hunt migrating sardines in the Indian Ocean, and thousands of turtles make the annual pilgrimage to lay their eggs in Costa Rica(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Camera crews journey to the deepest parts of the world's oceans to explore the lives of their inhabitants, including fearsome predators with huge teeth, strange jellyfish and deep-sea sharks that lurk among the gigantic submerged mountains and volcanos. Presented by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Predators and prey in the open oceans come under the scrutiny of David Attenborough. Marlin track down a shoal of sardines as a sei whale scoops up the leftovers, dolphins herd mackerel and attract the attention of a sailfish with the resonance of their sonar, and giant sunfish queue up to allow other species, including seagulls, to feed on their parasites(n)

programme.title: Guitar Heroes at the BBC
programme.desc: Status Quo play Pictures of Matchstick Men on Top of the Pops in 1968, the Who perform Long Live Rock in the Old Grey Whistle Test studio, Dire Straits deliver Tunnel of Love and Lynyrd Skynyrd bring a taste of the American South with Sweet Home Alabama. The programme also features rare performances from George Benson, Leo Kottke, Link Wray and Tom Petty(n)

programme.title: Guitar Heroes at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive footage from BBC music shows of the 1970s. Including electric performances by the Rolling Stones, Eric Clapton, David Bowie, Motorhead, Nazareth, AC/DC and George Thorogood, as well as acoustic material by Michael Chapman, Horslips and Paco de Lucia(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Britain
programme.sub-title: Secrets of the Tower of London
programme.desc: An exploration of British landmarks, beginning with a look at the Tower of London - which has served as a castle, a jail and a place of execution(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Britain
programme.desc: The series examining some of the UK's landmarks continues, with a look at the history of subterranean London, uncovering Roman remains, plague pits and secret bunkers under the capital's streets(n)

programme.title: Monster Ships
programme.desc: The work carried out by ONE Columba, which is capable of carrying 14,000 shipping containers, but has the dangerous challenge of navigating twisting rivers and ancient ports(n)

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The sabotaging efforts of Peugeot in Nazi-occupied France, where the bosses and workers cooperated closely with the Resistance to hinder the use of military vehicles by the Nazis(n)

programme.title: Time Team Specials
programme.sub-title: Henry VIII's Lost Palaces: A Time Team Special
programme.desc: In a programme made in 2009 to mark the 500th anniversary of Henry VIII's accession to the throne, Tony Robinson and the team track the legacy of his great building works. The monarch inherited a handful of medieval castles, but his desire to copy the achievements of the European Renaissance prompted him to sponsor 55 construction projects, some of which he designed(n)

programme.title: Battle Factory
programme.desc: A look at the production of Swiss army knives, sub-machine guns, body armour and laser sights(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Napoleon
programme.desc: Andrew Roberts' definitive account of Napoleon Bonaparte's life continues, with an insight into the French political leader's transformation into an emperor and a global statesman. Picking up his story in 1805, a year after Napoleon has been crowned King of Italy, Andrew visits the site of the Battle of Austerlitz, widely considered to be one of the most important engagements of the Napoleonic Wars. There, on the plains of Moravia, Bonaparte's Grande Armee defeated the combined forces of Russia's Tsar Alexander I and the Holy Roman Emperor Francis II - despite commanding a much smaller force. The victory marked the beginning of the rise of the Napoleonic Empire, but Andrew reveals that securing his place on the global stage came with a price, as Napoleon made compromises that relied on his trusting nature - a personality trait that would play a part in his ultimate downfall(n)

programme.title: After Hitler
programme.desc: The conclusion of the two-part documentary examines the Nuremberg Trials, the division of Germany, and the founding of the United Nations(n)

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the wildlife eking out an existence in and around the scorching wilderness of the Sahara Desert, where only the toughest survive. It's a constant struggle against dehydration, as zebra battle over dwindling rivers, camels search for water and tiny barn swallows navigate thousands of square miles of barren sand to find a life-saving oasis. The naked mole rat avoids the heat by living a subterranean existence, while the silver ant is able to take on the midday sun - but needs a 'spacesuit' to do it(n)

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the vast and diverse continent, aiming to show its landscapes and wildlife as they have never been seen before. He begins in the Kalahari desert, Africa's south-west corner, where clever meerkats are outsmarted by a wily bird's use of mimicry, a solitary rhino is seen to exhibit previously unknown sociable behaviours, terrifying insects prey on baby birds and the rarest fish in the world is filmed for the first time. In the neighbouring Namib desert, which receives almost no rain, the competition for scant resources can be fierce, as proven by a ferocious fight for supremacy between two giraffes(n)

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: David Attenborough continues his exploration of the continent in east Africa, which is a haven for life and supports a higher density of large mammals than anywhere else on Earth. From dense forests to snow-capped peaks, steamy swamps to endless savannah, it's also a land of unpredictable extremes, where creatures must adapt to survive. In Tanzania, a tiny lizard risks its life to eat flies from the face of a sleepy lion. The nesting habits of the 4ft shoebill are also captured on camera - the giant bird has two chicks, but due to sibling rivalry only one will survive. On the plains of Amboseli, a baby elephant is near to death in the drought, so its mother leaves the herd to stay with her offspring until the bitter end(n)

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the varied creatures of the Congo rainforest, one of the most competitive habitats on Earth. A sweet-toothed chimpanzee uses a complex array of tools to access honey from a beehive, the elusive picathartes bird feeds unsuspecting frogs to its dinosaur-like chicks and a massive female python reveals her maternal side as she sunbathes to incubate her eggs. Tiny leaf-folding frogs battle it out kung fu style, and as darkness falls the forest elephants gather to face their rivals(n)

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: David Attenborough describes some of the wildlife spectacles of southern Africa, starting out in a little-explored rainforest discovered less than a decade ago by scientists who spotted it on satellite maps. There, thousands of butterflies dance for the right to mate, while on a tiny island off Mozambique, green turtles emerge from eggs and begin a hazardous journey to the water - with pied crows and hungry crabs eager to get them. Cameras also capture one of the largest gatherings of great white sharks on the planet as 30 of the predators circle around a dead whale(n)

programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the wildlife eking out an existence in and around the scorching wilderness of the Sahara Desert, where only the toughest survive. It's a constant struggle against dehydration, as zebra battle over dwindling rivers, camels search for water and tiny barn swallows navigate thousands of square miles of barren sand to find a life-saving oasis. programme.title: Saluting Dad's Army
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong goes behind the scenes to discover just how Dad's Army was made and meets locals from the Norfolk town of Thetford, which stood in for Walmington-on-Sea. Plus, he hears from the expert professionals who brought Jimmy Perry and David Croft's ambitious ideas to the screen(n)

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: General von Klinkerhoffen visits the vineyard where Rene and the Resistance are working, and jeopardises their scheme to return the British airmen home(n)

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.sub-title: Desperate Doings in the Dungeon (aka The Rescue of Frau Kinkenrotten)
programme.desc: Herr Flick is imprisoned in General Von Klinkerhoffen's dungeon, leaving Rene to rescue him, and there is a plan afoot to micro-photograph Hitler's invasion plans(n)

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Gruber rescues René from his cell, while Herr Flick orders Helga to collect evidence of the supposed plot to blow up Hitler(n)

programme.title: Flying Scotsman with Robson Green
programme.desc: The presenter spends a year working with the team of engineers who have been commissioned to rebuild the most famous steam engine in the world. Starting in February, Robson is given the task of cutting off the front end of the Scotsman and welding on a whole piece, and sets out to Durham, where he discovers how the invention of the steam engine helped to change the world. After a year in the workshop, the iconic train is ready for its first test run, and Robson realises a lifelong dream and gets to ride on the footplate as it sets off(n)

programme.title: WW2 - Battles for Europe
programme.desc: The Allies reached the last great natural barrier between their forces and the German heartland. If they could get across the River Rhine, the war would be won(n)

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: The unfortunate fate of the world's first tank, which failed to make it onto the battlefield, and the case of the Japanese soldier who continued fighting - until 1974(n)

programme.title: WW2 Price of Empire
programme.desc: Archive pictures and eyewitness testimony reveal how the Allies finally achieved victory, following a series of successes in Europe and the Far East(n)

programme.title: WW2 Price of Empire
programme.desc: Reflections on the final days of the Second World War and the surrender of the Axis powers, including how empires fell and the political map of the world was remade(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past
programme.desc: US writer Janice Bennett investigates claims that the Holy Chalice of Valencia is in fact the Holy Grail(n)

programme.title: WWI Aces Falling
programme.desc: The story of Edward Mannock and James McCudden, the British fighter pilots who rose from modest backgrounds to become two of the country's greatest First World War aces. But as their tally of victories grew, so did their chances of dying. Timewatch recounts Mannock and McCudden's battle to survive against the odds - and the 90-year mystery surrounding their deaths(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Rare footage of the elusive animals that live in the unforgiving environment of the world's mountain ranges. A female snow leopard with a cub ends up caught between two rival males, grizzly bears rub themselves up against trees in an attempt to shed their winter fur, and golden eagles hunt over snow-capped peaks(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of plants and animals that live in jungles, one of the hardest environments to thrive in, including strange fungi and creatures that glow in the dark which have never been captured on film before. The programme also includes recordings of caimans and the jaguars that prey on them, freshwater dolphins that swim through flooded forests, undergrowth-dwelling frogs that have to defend themselves against wasps, and the Indri, the world's largest living lemur, which inhabits the forests of Madagascar(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of animals that live in the desert, including a pride of desert lions that risks hunting a giraffe several times their size. The film also features an immense swarm of locusts, sand grouse flying for 120 miles to gather water for their offspring, and an unprecedented recording of a tiny bat defending itself against one of the world's deadliest scorpions(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Wildlife that inhabit the world's grasslands, areas that are struck by some of the most dramatic seasonal changes seen in the world. The programme features footage of Brazil's giant anteaters and Asian saiga antelope, as well as lions engaging in ferocious battles with buffalo in the flooded Okavango, bee-eaters taking advantage of elephants to help them catch insects, and caribou embarking on migrations across the freezing northern tundra, stalked every step of the way by predatory Arctic wolves(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of wild animals that have made a home in cities, including leopards on the streets of Mumbai, peregrine falcons hunting among New York's skyscrapers, hyenas that live peacefully alongside the people of Harar, macaques in Jaipur, and langurs in Jodhpur, which are regarded as having great religious significance by the citizens. The programme also examines how some animals are struggling to survive in urban environments, and questions how cities can be built to serve their needs as well as those of the human inhabitants(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of wildlife that inhabit remote islands, revealing the advantages and disadvantages of the creatures' habitat. Three-toed sloths in the Caribbean and nesting albatross enjoy a peaceful existence untroubled by predators, but other animals are not so fortunate. Marine iguanas in the Galapagos Islands are preyed on by racer snakes from birth, while penguins on the Antarctic island of Zavodovski risk being battered against the rocks by fierce waves(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Rare footage of the elusive animals that live in the unforgiving environment of the world's mountain ranges. A female snow leopard with a cub ends up caught between two rival males, grizzly bears rub themselves up against trees in an attempt to shed their winter fur, and golden eagles hunt over snow-capped peaks(n)
